channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: The great loops debate and the state of AI engineering
item.description: The AI Engineer World’s Fair ended with a debate about loops, a report on the state of AI engineering, and closing keynotes focused on what to build next.

item.title: PACE: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Plausible and Actionable Counterfactual Explanations
item.description: arXiv:2607.01306v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Counterfactual explanations explain machine learning predictions by identifying minimal input changes that would alter a model's decision. Although many existing methods successfully generate prediction-changing alternatives, they often produce...

item.title: Auto-FL-Research: Agentic Search for Federated Learning Algorithms
item.description: arXiv:2607.01366v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated learning (FL) research often depends on many small but consequential algorithmic choices: optimizer variants, server aggregation rules, local training schedules, normalization, regularization, and model architecture. These choices are...

item.title: The Wiola Architecture for Efficient Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.01394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Wiola, a fully original Small Language Model (SLM) architecture built from first principles, sharing no structural lineage with any existing model family including GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, or Falcon. Wiola introduces five independently novel...

item.title: Agent4cs: A Multi-agent System for Code Summarization in Large Hierarchical Codebases
item.description: arXiv:2607.01425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding large, complex codebases, especially those with obfuscated structures and incomplete documentation, remains a significant challenge. Existing code summarization solutions often rely on a single language model or coding assistant like...

item.title: When Should Service Agents Reconsider? Difficulty-Routed Control in Customer-Service Operations
item.description: arXiv:2607.01426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous customer-service agents are shifting from conversational interfaces toward operational execution roles: they retrieve firm records, apply service policies, and execute backend writes such as refunds, cancellations, exchanges, order...

item.title: Multilayer Q-Matrix-Embedded Neural Network for Cognitive Diagnosis (M-QCDNet): Structure-Aware Deep Learning Architecture for Psychometric Interpretability
item.description: arXiv:2607.01278v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The research proposes a multilayer Q-matrix-embedded neural network for cognitive diagnosis (M-QCDNet), which integrates the structural interpretability of cognitive diagnostic models (CDMs) with the deep learning neural network (NN). M-QCDNet...

item.title: I\textsuperscript{2}RiMA: Spectral Riemannian Representation with Temporal Attention for Mental Stress Detection based on EEG Signals
item.description: arXiv:2607.01279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-subject EEG stress detection remains challenging because discriminative stress-related patterns are both subject-dependent and frequency-specific. Conventional Riemannian methods model spatial covariance mainly in the time domain, overlooking...

item.title: Fixed-Set Robustness in Programming by Example: Example Corruption and Semantic Partition Recovery
item.description: arXiv:2607.01280v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Programming-by-example systems infer programs from a small set of input-output examples. Robust PBE work usually models wrong examples as samples from a stochastic noise process and then minimizes an expected or empirical loss. This paper studies a...

item.title: Domain Knowledge Based Temporal-Spatial Graph Convolution Network for ECG Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2607.01282v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In light of strides in Arti cial Intelligence (AI) and its wide spread application, challenges persist in the interpretability of AI models, particularly within specialized domains like healthcare, such as electro cardiograph (ECG) recognition. Rather...

item.title: Scaling Laws for Grid-Based Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search in High Dimensions
item.description: arXiv:2607.01283v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Grid-based approaches to approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search have been absent from modern scaling analyses. We present a systematic characterization of a multiprobe grid algorithm with respect to dataset size $N$ and dimensionality $d$. Our...

item.title: TokenScope: Token-Level Explainability and Interpretability for Code-Oriented Tasks in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.01235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding how Large Language Models (LLMs) make token-level decisions during code generation remains a major challenge for both researchers and practitioners. While recent tools provide insights into model internals or generation outcomes, they...

item.title: Safeguarding LLM Agents from Misalignment through Provenance Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2607.01236v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLM agents gain increasing access to powerful tools, ensuring that their actions are aligned with the user's intent becomes critical. When an agent's proposed tool invocation deviates from the user's intent -- a phenomenon called misalignment -- it...

item.title: Kara: Efficient Reasoning LLM Serving via Sliding-Window KV Cache Compression
item.description: arXiv:2607.01237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reasoning language models often generate long chain-of-thought (CoT), which accumulates a massive KV cache during the decoding phase and incurs high decoding latency and limited throughput. To address these issues, KV cache compression has emerged as...

item.title: SPARCLE: SPeaker-aware Aligned Representations via Contrastive Language Embeddings
item.description: arXiv:2607.01238v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in speech synthesis have shifted from phoneme representations to direct grapheme modeling. While phonemes address the one-to-many mapping between text and acoustics, they rely on grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) systems that fail to capture...

item.title: Breaking Safety at the Token Boundary: How BPE Tokenization Creates Exploitable Gaps in LLM Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2607.01239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Character-level perturbations bypass safety alignment in modern LLMs despite leaving prompts human-readable. We identify and test a central structural mechanism: BPE tokenization fragments safety-critical words into sub-word pieces, and the three...

item.title: XPENG MONA L03 Unveiled with Pre-Sales Starting at RMB 143,800
item.description: XPENG launches MONA L03, its first SUV under the MONA series, with pre-sales starting at RMB 143,800. Pure electric and extended-range versions available, featuring Turing AI chip with up to 1,500 TOPS compute. Global launch July 16, 2026 in Munich.

item.title: China’s Domestic AI in the First Half of 2026: From Compute Substitution to Training Closure
item.description: Meituan’s LongCat-2.0, trained entirely on domestic chips, marks a milestone as China’s AI ecosystem achieves full training closure on homegrown infrastructure

item.title: Tech Giants Flood Into Embodied Intelligence as the Battle Heats Up
item.description: Li Auto, Alibaba, ByteDance, and more join the embodied intelligence race, with Morgan Stanley projecting 2.8 million humanoid robot sales in China by 2026

item.title: CAS Institute of Software Launches Reasoning Lens, Making AI Model Thinking Processes Visible
item.description: Chinese Academy of Sciences researchers develop Reasoning Lens, a system that visualizes and diagnoses AI reasoning chains to address the transparency burden of large models

item.title: Xiaomi 18 Series Set for September Launch with 2nm Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen6 Chip
item.description: Xiaomi’s upcoming flagship phone series will debut Qualcomm’s first 2nm chip with a split standard and Pro variant strategy to manage rising costs

item.title: Issues with network performance in ENAM and WNAM, US
item.description: Jul 3, 01:36 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 3, 01:10 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified issues with network performance in ENAM and WNAM, US. Customers using Argo Smart Routing in the affected areas may be impacted.

item.title: Vercel's Andrew Qu on why agents are a new kind of software
item.description: The Vercel Chief of Software explains how its agent framework, eve, was created — and why skills, sandboxes and agent-readable websites now matter.

item.title: Network Congestion in Newark, NJ (EWR)
item.description: Jul 2, 22:12 UTC Resolved - Between 20:18-21:30 UTC customers reaching Newark, NJ (EWR) would have experienced an elevated number of network errors due to congestion. The impacted services would have included Cloudflare Zero Trust and R2.

item.title: The website of the future may assemble itself for every visitor
item.description: Adobe is experimenting with “agentic sites” that generate pages around an individual user’s intent. At AIEWF, we talked to Carlos Sanchez about the Web's future.

item.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks...

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description: Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic. Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said this week it had reduced the network's pool of usable...

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description: Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access. "Although tactics differ between affiliates, common patterns emerged in tradecraft through use of legitimate Remote Management and...

item.title: Incident with Pages
item.description: Jul 2, 16:56 UTC Update - We are investigating reports of slow and failing Pages deployments. Access to Pages is unaffected. Jul 2, 16:54 UTC Investigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Pages

item.title: Realtimekit Issues
item.description: Jul 2, 16:28 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 2, 15:59 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 2, 15:05 UTC Investigating - Some customers may experience issues joining realtimekit meetings from India.

item.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
item.description: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how...

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description: This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots. Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a small gap and finds a way through. This is not one big break. It is small permissions, weak checks,...

item.title: New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure
item.description: Hackers are targeting NetScaler appliances using public PoC code to retrieve arbitrary memory content in the HTTP response. The post New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Skill engineering and the case against one-shot AI design
item.description: Paul Bakaus talks to us about Impeccable, human judgment in a 'loopmaxxing' era, and why agents still need people to steer them.

item.title: Cloudflare Workers build delays
item.description: Jul 2, 13:46 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jul 2, 13:40 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with Cloudflare Workers build delays which potentially impacts multiple customers. Further detail will be provided as more information becomes...

item.title: How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development
item.description: As AI-generated code becomes commonplace, CISOs need new audit strategies to measure developer practices, govern AI tool usage, and identify software risks before they reach production. The post How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description: The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API. "In this campaign, the attackers focused their attention on corporate email communications hosted on Gmail,...

item.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
item.description: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is...

item.title: FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Researchers say credentials harvested from hundreds of thousands of FortiGate firewalls are being used to facilitate ransomware attacks by the INC and Lynx operations. The post FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give...

item.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across enterprise environments, the governance model built for humans develops structural blind spots that...

item.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm
item.description: Anthropic said Tuesday night that its AI model called Claude Fable 5 is now widely available. The post Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cisco Confirms In-the-Wild Exploitation of Unified CM Vulnerability
item.description: A PoC exploit has been available since public disclosure, and the first exploitation attempts were observed last week. The post Cisco Confirms In-the-Wild Exploitation of Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
item.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s...

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: another quiet day.

item.title: Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in ENAM and WNAM, US
item.description: Jul 2, 02:29 UTC Identified - We are still seeing some impact.The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 2, 01:39 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 2, 01:03 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 2, 01:19 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 2, 00:58 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 2, 00:38 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
item.description: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

item.title: Building more resilient CNI: what industry pen testers told us
item.description: Pen testers suggest what organisations can do to make their job more difficult.

item.title: Delays in copilot budget limits resets for some users
item.description: Jul 1, 10:51 UTC Investigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Copilot
